standout_lines: Raising kids under five is a mystery, and nobody here pretends otherwise. Kids are loud in the lobby and welcome to be. If you have questions about any of it, Teacher Donna reads every email.
A quiet room off the gathering space with somewhere to sit, feed a baby, or settle a fussy one. If you need a minute to breathe, take it. Nobody is counting.
Blocks, crayons, and floor time with teachers who know your kid by name. Belonging starts here, before any of it has words.
before you were even born, god loved you first
BEGINNING WITH A STRONG SENSE OF BELONGING
problem_lines: We want all of our kids to grow up with a sense that God's love is generous, inclusive, and affirming of all human beings.

ParaTots meets Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive in Redlands, for kids ages 0 to 4. Teacher Donna coordinates the room each week. The whole gathering follows at 10:00, so drop-off and pick-up stay simple.

directives.note: The body uses '51 W Olive in Redlands' and '9:15 AM' as literal globals/facts. Per snippets consistency rule 13, recurring globals (address, primary_service_time) should be tokenized rather than hardcoded in body slots. Same untokenized literals recur in section 3 body and section 2 items. Confirm whether these should be replaced with snippet tokens (e.g., {{address}}, {{paratots_service_time}}) or accepted as informational logistics.

'Generous, Inclusive, Affirming Love' is a triad heading. Drafter's voice_notes correctly argue the triad is the church's own intentional language (lifted verbatim from atom 73a9658c), so this passes the triad test — but as a standalone heading without the 'God's love is' lead-in, it reads slightly closer to a marketing tagline than the atom's full sentence does. Consider whether the heading lands as a clean label or if strategist wants it tightened to a shorter cadence.

Outline voice_anchor was 'Saturday evenings in Redlands' but draft correctly used Saturday 9:15 AM per the fact row. Drafter flagged this honestly in validation.flags. No change needed to copy; strategist should confirm the outline anchor was a stale pattern reference, not a service-time error.

critique.summary: Strong, dignified ParaTots draft that honors the Paradox voice — quiet, unsentimental, parent-respecting. All four atoms land: both verbatim prose snippets (94df26ac, 2528ca66) preserved exactly with casing intact; the voice_sample atom (73a9658c) appears verbatim in section 1 body; the voice_rule atom (be43f59d) is used as tonal anchor without being quoted, which is correct per the lift_phrase treatment. Mechanical scan is clean: no em-dashes, no AI clichés, no banned church clichés ('come as you are' was honored in posture but not quoted, per drafter's deviation_note). The 'generous, inclusive, and affirming' triad is the church's own intentional vocabulary, not filler — passes the triad test. Hero is appropriately quiet (no buttons, no enthusiasm). Section 4 close is a standout: 'Raising kids under five is a mystery, and nobody here pretends otherwise' + verbatim exemplar 'Kids are loud in the lobby and welcome to be.' Persona fit: Lena and Jordan are well-served by the 'generous, inclusive, affirming' framing and the non-performative tone; Marcus is implicit but the no-sell posture serves him. Headings are clean labels. Primary CTA is specific ('Plan a Visit', 'Email Teacher Donna'). Minor concern: globals (address, service time, email) appear as literal strings in body slots — flag for snippets-consistency review, but informational tagline + logistics-section exceptions likely apply. Recommend ship with one strategist skim on the section 2 voice_anchor drift and section 4 heading cadence."
